content.body: I think we generally make events bubble, but it might be worth investigating some other recent introductions.
But also, I wish we'd focus on fixing all the existing issues with customizable <select>. There's way too many.

Generallly events that relate to interaction bubble (click, keydown, change, submit) and other events (media progress, load, error, formdata) don't. though this has some exceptions that are not always obvious. "beforefillter" should perhaps bubble if you want to be able to handle it at the form level for example.
(this is by judging from existing events, not from some official guidance)
annevk: mind looking at https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/12266 when you get a chance? It has been ready for review for a while and is quite small
content.formatted_body: annevk: mind looking at https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/12266 when you get a chance? It has been ready for review for a while and is quite small

content.body: does the note "no elements will be added to HTML, SVG, or MathML with hyphen-containing local names going forward" apply to attribute names as well?
ie is it html's purview to leave unhyphenated names to html and hyphenated names to devs?
* ie is it html's purview to leave unhyphenated attributes to html and hyphenated attributes to devs as well?

m.new_content.body: ie is it html's purview to leave unhyphenated attributes to html and hyphenated attributes to devs as well?

content.body: aria and svg have hyphenated attribute names. The official userspace for attribute is `data-*` afaik
content.formatted_body: aria and svg have hyphenated attribute names. The official userspace for attribute is data-* afaik

content.body: but there is a "consistency" somewhat unofficial rule to keep names concatenated without a separator, see https://www.w3.org/TR/design-principles/#casing-rules

> <@noamr:matrix.org> aria and svg have hyphenated attribute names. The official userspace for attribute is `data-*` afaik

I guess with custom attributes that might change to more generally allow hyphenated userland attributes?

I think we generally shouldn't add new hyphenated ones as built-ins personally.

In reply to @noamr:matrix.org
aria and svg have hyphenated attribute names. The official userspace for attribute is data-* afaik

I guess with custom attributes that might change to more generally allow hyphenated userland attributes?

I think we generally shouldn't add new hyphenated ones as built-ins personally.

content.body: Yea I agree that making that semi-official API design principle something more official can be useful (with `aria-` being an exception)
content.formatted_body: Yea I agree that making that semi-official API design principle something more official can be useful (with aria- being an exception)

content.body: (Also `http-equiv` and `accept-charset` if we want to be archeological about it)
content.formatted_body: (Also http-equiv and accept-charset if we want to be archeological about it)
